zh.Allerdale below Derwent, Cumberland, Eng: 英國英格蘭波藍德阿勒爾達爾 貝洛 德文特
zh.lifeSummary: Lady Euphemia de Livington於1230年出生在英國英格蘭波藍德阿勒爾達爾 貝洛 德文特。她和Richard De Kirkbride至少有2個兒子。她去世於1299年在英國英格蘭波藍德吉可布利德享年69歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Livington
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from a place in Lothian, originally named in Middle English as Levingston. The placename derives from the Middle English personal name Leving (genitive Levinges) + Middle English, Older Scots toun ‘town, village, settlement’.
Irish: surname adopted as equivalent of Gaelic Ó Duinnshléibhe and Mac Duinnshléibhe (see Dunleavy ).

variants.name: Dunleavy
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Aidan, Connor, John Patrick, Siobhan.

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Duinnshléibhe or Ó Duinnshléibhe ‘son of Donn Sléibhe’, a personal name composed of the elements donn ‘brown-haired man’ or ‘chieftain’ + sléibhe ‘of the mountain’, meaning ‘chieftain (literally, brown one) of the mountain’.

nameMeanings.name: Euphemia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of a Late Greek name derived from eu ‘well, good’ + phēnai ‘to speak’. This was the name of various early saints, notably a virgin martyr said to have been burned at the stake at Chalcedon in 307 . It had a limited popularity in some regions in the 16th and 17th centuries and returned to favour in the Victorian period, especially in the pet form Effie . It is little used at present.

en.lifeSummary: Lady Euphemia de Livington was born in 1230, in Allerdale below Derwent, Cumberland, England, United Kingdom. She had at least 2 sons with Richard De Kirkbride. She died in 1299, in Kirkbride, Cumberland, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 69.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Euphemia de Livingtonは1230年にイギリスイギリスクウンバーランドアラーダル ベロウ デルウエントで生まれた。彼女はRichard De Kirkbrideとの間に少なくとも2人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1299年にイギリスイギリスクウンバーランドキルクブリドで69歳で死去した。

ko.Allerdale below Derwent, Cumberland, Eng: 영국 잉글랜드 c벌랜드 앨레르데일 브로 더웬트
ko.lifeSummary: Lady Euphemia de Livington(은)는 1230년 영국 잉글랜드 c벌랜드 앨레르데일 브로 더웬트에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Richard De Kirkbride(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1299년 영국 잉글랜드 c벌랜드 커k브라이드에서 69세의 나이로 사망했다.
